approach.reasoning:
The agent framed the problem correctly: used ERCOT Houston Hub real-time LMPs for the summer months and computed dispatch cost as heat rate × Henry Hub gas price, dispatching when LMP > dispatch cost (spark spread > 0). Tool usage was appropriate (queried the ERCOT LMP table, pulled EIA Henry Hub data, and performed programmatic analysis). Heat-rate conversion was handled correctly (10,000 Btu/kWh → 10 MMBtu/MWh).

Minor issues/limitations that prevent an expert-level score: the agent used monthly Henry Hub averages rather than more granular delivered or daily gas prices (or hub-to-hub basis differentials) which can materially affect short-term dispatch decisions; it ignored other realistic costs and constraints called out in the ground truth (variable O&M, startup/shutdown costs, minimum run times, inter-temporal constraints, and nodal/constraint/congestion impacts). Also September was omitted (acceptable per instructions, but noted). Overall the approach is logical and consistent with the simplistic theoretical dispatch objective, with reasonable tool use, but simplified assumptions limit real-world accuracy.

accuracy.reasoning: The agent provided no substantive answer to evaluate. The expected (ground-truth) logic and revenue estimates are: dispatch when LMP > gas_price * heat_rate, and a 1 MW CT should yield ~$24,207±10% (including September) or ~$21,532±10% (excluding September) based on 2024 monthly Henry Hub averages. Because the agent gave no numerical values, no dispatch rule, and no market/node/settlement details, the response fails on numerical correctness, factual alignment (ERCOT Houston Hub real-time LMPs and Henry Hub conversion with 10,000 Btu/kWh), and completeness. Under the specified tolerance (≤2 absolute error or ≤10% relative), an empty answer cannot meet any criterion, so accuracy_score is 0.0.

sources.reasoning: The agent provided a correct high-level dispatch rule (dispatch when LMP > dispatch cost) but did not explicitly cite any authoritative data sources. For this energy‑market analysis the expected authoritative sources would include: (1) Henry Hub gas price time series for summer 2024 from CME/ICE (futures/realized), EIA, or Platts/Argus; (2) ERCOT Houston Hub real‑time LMPs (ERCOT Market Information/MMS/Market Reports or ERCOT Public Data Portal) for June–September 2024; and (3) clear unit/heat‑rate conversion guidance (heat rate in Btu/kWh to MMBtu/MWh: 10,000 Btu/kWh = 10 MMBtu/MWh so dispatch cost $/MWh = 10 * $/MMBtu). The answer omitted these citations and did not show the conversion explicitly, nor did it state whether fuel basis adjustments (basis between Henry Hub and Houston gas receipt point) or pipeline/transport premiums were considered. It also did not mention typical omitted costs that materially affect dispatch decisions (variable O&M, start‑up costs, minimum run times, ramp constraints, outages, or ancillary revenues). Given the lack of explicit, authoritative source citations and missing practical adjustments, source validity is low (2). To be acceptable, the agent should reference ERCOT RT LMP data and a credible Henry Hub price source (or internal equivalent), show the unit conversion, and note any basis/transport or market settlement differences.
